risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "During year 1 of the study, patients were randomized (1:1:1) to receive subcutaneous injections of placebo, peginterferon beta-1a at a dose of 125 μg every 2 weeks, or peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg every 4 weeks. At the end of year 1, patients on placebo were re-randomized to peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg dosed every 2 weeks or every 4 weeks." Comment: The report states that participants were randomized, but it does not describe the method used to generate the allocation sequence (e.g., computer-generated list, random numbers, blocks, or stratification). Under the handbook criteria, 'randomized' alone without further context is insufficient to confirm a truly random sequence, so this remains unclear.

_evidence.method_quote: ADVANCE was a 2 year, phase 3, multicentre, randomized, double-blind, parallel group study with a 1 year placebo-controlled period. During year 1 of the study, patients were randomized (1:1:1) to receive subcutaneous injections of placebo, peginterferon beta-1a at a dose of 125 μg every 2 weeks, or peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg every 4 weeks. At the end of year 1, patients on placebo were re-randomized to peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg dosed every 2 weeks or every 4 weeks.
_evidence.method_summary: Patients were randomized in a 1:1:1 parallel-group design, with placebo participants re-randomized at year 1.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "During year 1 of the study, patients were randomized (1:1:1) to receive subcutaneous injections of placebo, peginterferon beta-1a at a dose of 125 μg every 2 weeks, or peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg every 4 weeks." Comment: The article states that participants were randomized, but it does not describe any allocation concealment mechanism (e.g., central allocation, sequentially numbered opaque sealed envelopes, or identical numbered containers). Therefore, the recruiter/enroller's ability to foresee the next assignment cannot be ruled out, but is not shown, so the correct judgement is Unclear risk.

_evidence.concealment_quote: During year 1 of the study, patients were randomized (1:1:1) to receive subcutaneous injections of placebo, peginterferon beta-1a at a dose of 125 μg every 2 weeks, or peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg every 4 weeks.
_evidence.note: The article describes randomization and blinding, but does not describe how the allocation sequence was concealed from recruiters/enrollers.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "ADVANCE was a 2 year, phase 3, multicentre, randomized, double-blind, parallel group study" and "To maintain the blind, patients received either peginterferon beta-1a or placebo every 2 weeks. Those randomized to the peginterferon beta-1a dosed every 4 weeks group received alternate injections of placebo and peginterferon beta-1a every 2 weeks." Comment: The study explicitly states that it was double-blind and describes a specific masking procedure for both participants and treating personnel, making successful blinding plausible. The reported outcomes in this paper are objective PK/PD and immunogenicity measures, which are unlikely to be influenced by knowledge of allocation, further supporting a low risk judgment for performance bias.

_evidence.blinding_quote: ADVANCE was a 2 year, phase 3, multicentre, randomized, double-blind, parallel group study with a 1 year placebo-controlled period. During year 1 of the study, patients were randomized (1:1:1) to receive subcutaneous injections of placebo, peginterferon beta-1a at a dose of 125 μg every 2 weeks, or peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg every 4 weeks. To maintain the blind, patients received either peginterferon beta-1a or placebo every 2 weeks. Those randomized to the peginterferon beta-1a dosed every 4 weeks group received alternate injections of placebo and peginterferon beta-1a every 2 weeks. At the end of year 1, patients on placebo were re-randomized to peginterferon beta-1a 125 μg dosed every 2 weeks or every 4 weeks. Thus, during year 2, all patients received dose-frequency blinded peginterferon beta-1a.
_evidence.blinding_method: To maintain the blind, patients received either peginterferon beta-1a or placebo every 2 weeks; the every 4 weeks group received alternate injections of placebo and peginterferon beta-1a every 2 weeks; during year 2, all patients received dose-frequency blinded peginterferon beta-1a.
outcome_categories: immunogenicity (anti-PEG antibodies, anti-IFN BAbs, anti-IFN NAbs)
objective_outcomes: immunogenicity (anti-PEG antibodies, anti-IFN BAbs, anti-IFN NAbs)

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The PK and PD parameters were calculated by non-compartmental analysis using Phoenix WinNonLin software (Pharsight, Sunnyvale, CA, USA) based on intensive data." Comment: The reported outcomes for this study are PK, PD, and immunogenicity assays, all of which are objective lab-derived measures assessed by automated/validated systems rather than subjective judgement. The outcomes were not dependent on an unblinded clinician or patient self-report, so knowledge of allocation would be unlikely to influence measurement.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: The PK and PD parameters were calculated by non-compartmental analysis using Phoenix WinNonLin software (Pharsight, Sunnyvale, CA, USA) based on intensive data.

outcomes.label: anti-PEG antibodies / anti-IFN BAbs / anti-IFN NAbs

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The article does not provide CONSORT-style post-randomization flow or arm-level randomized vs analysed numbers for the outcomes of interest. It states, "A full description of participant flow in the ADVANCE study ... have been published previously" and only reports that "Forty-four patients consented to the intensive PK and PD subgroup ... and had sufficient time point samples for evaluation of PK and PD parameters." Comment: Because the analysed outcome denominators, losses to follow-up, and reasons for missing data are not reported in this article, the amount and balance of attrition cannot be determined; per the review guidance, this supports Unclear rather than High risk.

_evidence.notes: The article states: "A full description of participant flow in the ADVANCE study ... have been published previously" and reports only the intensive PK/PD subgroup: "Forty-four patients consented to the intensive PK and PD subgroup ... four male and 21 female patients received peginterferon beta-1a (every 2 weeks n = 12; every 4 weeks n = 13) and had sufficient time point samples for evaluation of PK and PD parameters." No CONSORT-style flow or arm-level randomised/analysed/lost numbers for the analysed outcomes are provided in this article.
